sections.paragraph:
f. (from the root רָמָה Pi.)-

(1) a letting down or relaxing of the hands, indolence. (This notion of the root nearly approaches to the cogn. רָפָה. Arab. رمى VIII. to be slack, and remiss, spoken of any affair.) Pro 12:24. כַּף רְמִיָּה a remiss hand, idle, Pro 10:4. Adv. negligently, Jer 48:10.

(2) deception, fraud, Job 13:7. לְשֹׁון רְמִיָּת a fraudulent tongue, Psa 120:2, Psa 120:3. קֶשֶׁת רְמִיָּה a deceitful bow, one which shoots untruly, Hos 7:16 poet. deceptive archers, who deceive by a false flight, Psa 78:57.
